picks.name: Dell Technologies Inc.
picks.thesis: DELL is down 13% from its 52-week high while earnings grew 34%/yr — a sentiment-driven pullback, not fundamental deterioration. Quality looks intact (healthy margins, durable revenue, long-term uptrend), and it's now at or below fair value ($409.94 vs $968.03, low confidence). Valued with DCF and Comps.

methods.reason: Not meaningful: this is a growth-oriented company that reinvests and returns cash mainly via buybacks rather than dividends (yield 0.3%, payout 13%), so a dividend-only model understates it — DDM excluded
